body_txt: A common way to break someone's spirit is with some horrible truth , but sometimes a person seeking to hurt someone might choose to tell a horrible lie instead. Some common reasons why a person might choose to lie is because the truth would uplift the person instead, a person needing to believe the lie in order to be manipulated, or turning friends against one another by telling hurtful lies about each other. There are a number of scenarios that would lead to someone telling such a lie; a Hope Crusher might resort to telling lies because the truth might create hope instead. A person who wants to drive someone to suicide might tell lies in order to push them over the edge. A cult member who wants to recruit people and/or control its members might tell potential recruits and members lies in order to isolate them from non-cult members. Naturally, said lies will almost always get exposed. Perhaps the person the liar is trying to break might see through the lies by pointing out why said lies can't be true. Other times the deceived's friends/family might reveal the truth to them. Other times the liar might accidentally say or do something that causes the deceived to realize that they've been duped. Frequent users of this trope tend to be the most loathsome Hate Sinks of the cast, for the simple reason that people don't like liars, let alone one who crafts lies specifically to hurt people (as opposed to make someone face an ugly truth). The Dirty Coward , the Slimeball , and the Manipulative Bastard are the ones most likely to resort to this. Sub-Trope of Break Them by Talking . See also Batman Gambit and Scare 'Em Straight . Compare Malicious Slander , which is lying to other people about someone, and Gaslighting , which involves convincing people that their own memories are a lie. Contrast Awful Truth and Villain Reveals the Secret when a person is broken by the truth, and Villains Never Lie , which this trope can sometimes act as a subversion of. Also contrast Lying to Protect Your Feelings , Motivational Lie , and Let Them Die Happy when someone tells a lie to make protect or soothe someone's feelings. No Recent Examples, Please! , as there are numerous and controversial examples of this happening in real life.
